content: According to Acts 1:8, when the Holy Spirit comes upon the disciples, what happens?
explanation: Acts 1:8 — They receive power and become witnesses in Jerusalem, Judea, Samaria, and to the ends of the earth.
options: They receive power and become witnesses everywhere
They return to their old jobs
They speak the tongues of angels

content: On the day of Pentecost, the disciples were filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak what?
explanation: Acts 2:4 — They began to speak in other tongues as the Spirit gave them utterance.

content: By what name was the lame man at the Beautiful Gate healed?
explanation: Acts 3:6 — 'In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, rise up and walk!'

content: According to Acts 4:12, in whom is salvation?
explanation: Acts 4:12 — There is no other name under heaven given among men by which we must be saved.

content: What was the chief purpose of the seven chosen in Acts 6?
explanation: Acts 6:2-4 — So the apostles could devote themselves to prayer and the ministry of the word.

content: On what road did Saul (Paul) meet the Lord?
explanation: Acts 9:3-6 — On the road to Damascus, the Lord appeared and called Saul.

content: What Scripture did Philip explain to the Ethiopian eunuch?
explanation: Acts 8:32-35 — Beginning with this Scripture, he told him the good news about Jesus.

content: What did Peter's vision of the sheet from heaven teach?
explanation: Acts 10:15 — What God has made clean, do not call common.
options: Do not call common what God has made clean

content: How was Peter rescued from prison?
explanation: Acts 12:7-10 — An angel of the Lord appeared, and the chains fell from his hands.
options: The doors opened on their own

content: At Antioch, who did the Holy Spirit set apart for the work?
explanation: Acts 13:2 — Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them.

content: What did the Jerusalem Council decide for Gentile believers?
explanation: Acts 15:28-29 — They decided to lay no greater burden on Gentile brothers.
options: Not require all the law of Moses; abstain from food sacrificed to idols, blood, things strangled, and sexual immorality

content: After what event did the Philippian jailer believe?
explanation: Acts 16:26-34 — An earthquake opened the prison doors; he believed and his whole household was baptized.

content: At the Areopagus, what did Paul preach to the Greeks?
explanation: Acts 17:24-31 — God the Creator, a call to repent, the day of judgment, and the resurrection.
options: God does not dwell in temples made by hands, and the resurrection

content: What was Apollos lacking, that Priscilla and Aquila explained more accurately?
explanation: Acts 18:25-26 — He knew only the baptism of John; they explained the way of God more accurately.
options: Knew only the baptism of John

content: At Ephesus, what caused many people to burn their books of magic?
explanation: Acts 19:11-19 — God did extraordinary miracles by the hands of Paul.

content: What happened to Eutychus during Paul's long sermon?
explanation: Acts 20:9-12 — Eutychus fell asleep and fell down; Paul embraced him and he came back to life.
options: Fell asleep, fell from the third floor, and was raised back to life

content: Why was Paul arrested in Jerusalem according to Acts 21?
explanation: Acts 21:28-29 — The Jews accused him of bringing a Greek into the temple and defiling the holy place.
options: Accused of bringing Gentiles into the temple

content: What did Paul say that divided the Pharisees and Sadducees?
explanation: Acts 23:6-8 — Pharisees believed in resurrection, angels, spirits; Sadducees did not.

content: Before King Agrippa, what did Paul say he had not opposed?
explanation: Acts 26:19 — I was not disobedient to the heavenly vision.

content: On the island of Malta, what happened to Paul after the viper bit him?
explanation: Acts 28:3-6 — Paul was unharmed, and the islanders changed their minds and called him a god.
options: Was unharmed; the people thought him a god

content: In Acts 2:4, 'They were all filled with the Holy Spirit.' What does the Greek for 'filled' mean?
explanation: Pimplemi (πίμπλημι) means 'filled to overflowing' — not just full but spilling over.

content: In Acts 9:15, the Lord said of Paul: 'He is a chosen instrument of mine.' What does the Greek for 'instrument' mean?
explanation: Skeuos (σκεῦος) means 'vessel, instrument' — referring to Paul as God's tool.

content: Before His ascension, what did Jesus promise the disciples would receive?
explanation: Acts 1:8 — When the Holy Spirit comes upon you, you will receive power and be My witnesses to the ends of the earth.
options: Power when the Holy Spirit comes upon them

content: On the day of Pentecost, what were the signs of the Holy Spirit's coming?
explanation: Acts 2:2-4 — A wind from heaven, tongues like fire on each one's head, and they spoke in other tongues by the power of the Spirit.
options: Mighty wind, tongues of fire, speaking in tongues

content: On Pentecost, what did Peter call the people to do?
explanation: Acts 2:38 — Peter called for repentance (turning), baptism in the name of the Lord, and receiving the Holy Spirit.
options: Repent and be baptized in the name of Jesus

content: On the way to where did Saul (later Paul) meet Jesus?
explanation: Acts 9:3 — On the road to Damascus, light shone from heaven and Jesus spoke to Saul.

content: What four things did the early church devote themselves to?
explanation: Acts 2:42 — Four pillars of the church: teaching, fellowship, breaking of bread, prayer.
options: The apostles' teaching, fellowship, the breaking of bread, and prayer

content: Peter said to the lame man at the temple gate: 'Silver and gold I do not have, but what I do have I give you: in the name of ___, rise up and walk'
explanation: Acts 3:6 — Healing power comes from the name of Jesus, not from money.

content: When forbidden to preach, the apostles answered: 'We must obey ___ rather than men'
explanation: Acts 5:29 — When human law contradicts God's law, we must choose to obey God.

content: What did Stephen, the first martyr, pray as he was being stoned?
explanation: Acts 7:60 — Like Jesus, Stephen forgave those who killed him. The highest standard of forgiveness.
options: Lord, do not hold this sin against them

content: Philip asked the Ethiopian eunuch: 'Do you understand what you are reading?' How did he answer?
explanation: Acts 8:31 — A teacher of Scripture is needed. The important role of a guide.
options: How can I, unless someone guides me?

content: Why were the Bereans commended when they heard Paul preach?
explanation: Acts 17:11 — The model: hear the message and check it against Scripture. Not blind belief.
options: Examined the Scriptures daily to see whether what Paul said was so

content: Paul quoted Jesus: 'It is more blessed to give than to ___'
explanation: Acts 20:35 — A saying of Jesus not in the Gospels, preserved by Paul.

content: After Jesus was taken up to heaven, who appeared to the disciples?
explanation: Acts 1:10-11 — 'Behold, two men stood by them in white robes, and said, Men of Galilee, why do you stand looking into heaven?'
options: Two men in white robes (two angels)

content: Who was chosen to replace Judas Iscariot to complete the twelve apostles?
explanation: Acts 1:26 — 'They cast lots for them, and the lot fell on Matthias, and he was numbered with the eleven apostles.'

content: What did Peter tell the crowd they must do after his Pentecost sermon?
explanation: Acts 2:38 — 'Repent and be baptized every one of you in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.'
options: Repent and be baptized in the name of Jesus, for the forgiveness of sins, and receive the Holy Spirit

content: What characterized the early church in Acts 2?
explanation: Acts 2:42 — 'They devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching and the fellowship, to the breaking of bread and the prayers.'
options: Devoted to apostles' teaching, fellowship, breaking of bread, and prayer

content: What did Peter say to the lame man at the Beautiful Gate?
explanation: Acts 3:6 — 'Silver and gold I have not, but what I have I give to you. In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, rise up and walk!'
options: I have no silver or gold; in the name of Jesus, rise up and walk

content: Acts 4:12 says there is only one name under heaven by which men must be saved — what name?
explanation: Acts 4:12 — 'And there is salvation in no one else, for there is no other name under heaven given among men by which we must be saved.'

content: How did the early church treat their possessions?
explanation: Acts 4:32 — 'Now the full number of those who believed were of one heart and soul, and no one said that any of the things that belonged to him was his own, but they had everything in common.'
options: Had everything in common; those with surplus gave to those in need

content: What couple fell dead for lying about their offering?
explanation: Acts 5:1-10 — 'A man named Ananias, with his wife Sapphira, sold a piece of property.' Both were struck dead by the Holy Spirit for lying.

content: When the Sanhedrin forbade the apostles to preach, how did Peter answer?
explanation: Acts 5:29 — 'Peter and the apostles answered, We must obey God rather than men.'
options: We must obey God rather than men

content: How many deacons did the church choose to oversee distribution to the widows?
explanation: Acts 6:3 — 'Therefore, brothers, pick out from among you seven men of good repute, full of the Spirit and of wisdom.'

content: Before being martyred, what did Stephen see when he looked into heaven?
explanation: Acts 7:55-56 — 'But he, full of the Holy Spirit, gazed into heaven and saw the glory of God, and Jesus standing at the right hand of God.' Note: Jesus was standing (not seated).
options: The glory of God and Jesus standing at God's right hand

content: Whom did the Spirit lead Philip to meet on the road to Gaza?
explanation: Acts 8:27 — 'An Ethiopian, a eunuch, a court official of Candace, queen of the Ethiopians... was sitting in his chariot reading the prophet Isaiah.'

content: Whom did Peter raise from the dead at Joppa?
explanation: Acts 9:40 — 'Peter said, Tabitha, arise. And she opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter she sat up.'

content: What did Peter realize about God at Cornelius's house?
explanation: Acts 10:34-35 — 'So Peter opened his mouth and said: Truly I understand that God shows no partiality, but in every nation anyone who fears him and does what is right is acceptable to him.'
options: Shows no partiality; in every nation those who fear Him and do right are acceptable

content: Where were the disciples first called 'Christians'?
explanation: Acts 11:26 — 'And in Antioch the disciples were first called Christians.'

content: How did Peter escape from prison in Acts 12?
explanation: Acts 12:7 — 'And behold, an angel of the Lord stood next to him, and a light shone in the cell. He struck Peter on the side and woke him.' The chains fell off Peter's hands.
options: An angel rescued him; chains fell off his hands

content: Why did King Herod (Agrippa I) die in Acts 12:23?
explanation: Acts 12:23 — 'Immediately an angel of the Lord struck him down, because he did not give God the glory, and he was eaten by worms and breathed his last.'
options: Struck by an angel for not giving God the glory; eaten by worms

content: Whom did the Holy Spirit tell the church at Antioch to set apart for missionary work?
explanation: Acts 13:2 — 'Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them.'

content: At Lystra, by what two Greek god names did the people call Paul and Barnabas?
explanation: Acts 14:12 — 'Barnabas they called Zeus, and Paul, Hermes, because he was the chief speaker.'

content: What did Paul and Silas do at midnight in the Philippian jail?
explanation: Acts 16:25-26 — 'About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God... and suddenly there was a great earthquake.'
options: Prayed and sang hymns to God (an earthquake came)
Waited for trial in the morning

content: The Philippian jailer asked, 'What must I do to be saved?' — what did Paul answer?
explanation: Acts 16:31 — 'Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved, you and your household.'
options: Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you and your household will be saved

content: What were the Bereans commended for doing daily to test Paul's preaching?
explanation: Acts 17:11 — 'Now these Jews were more noble than those in Thessalonica; they received the word with all eagerness, examining the Scriptures daily to see if these things were so.'
options: Examining the Scriptures daily to test the message

content: What couple did Paul meet at Corinth, who were tentmakers like him?
explanation: Acts 18:2-3 — 'And he found a Jew named Aquila... with his wife Priscilla... and because he was of the same trade he stayed with them and worked, for they were tentmakers by trade.'

content: At Ephesus, God did extraordinary miracles through Paul, even through what objects?
explanation: Acts 19:12 — 'So that even handkerchiefs or aprons that had touched his skin were carried away to the sick, and their diseases left them.'
options: Handkerchiefs and aprons that had touched Paul

content: What saying of Jesus did Paul quote in Acts 20:35 about giving?
explanation: Acts 20:35 — 'It is more blessed to give than to receive.'
options: It is more blessed to give than to receive
Blessed are the poor in spirit
Blessed are those who love their enemies

content: When Paul preached to Felix about righteousness, self-control, and judgment, what did Felix do?
explanation: Acts 24:25 — 'Felix was alarmed and said, Go away for the present. When I get an opportunity I will summon you.'
options: Was alarmed and said: leave for now; when convenient, I'll call you

content: What did King Agrippa II say to Paul after his testimony?
explanation: Acts 26:28 — 'Agrippa said to Paul, In a short time would you persuade me to be a Christian?'
options: In a short time would you persuade me to be a Christian?

content: On the island of Malta, what creature bit Paul on the hand without harming him?
explanation: Acts 28:3 — 'A viper came out because of the heat and fastened on his hand.' Paul shook it into the fire and was unharmed.
